title: MT queue starvation prevention (age-based priority + fair scheduling)
goal: Implement starvation prevention in the MicroTaskQueue so long-pending jobs do not get crowded out by a steady stream of fresh higher-tier jobs. Adds age-based priority boost + per-WP fair scheduling so no single WP can monopolize executor capacity.
expected_diff_shape: New Rust module src/backend/handshake_core/src/process_ledger/mt_scheduler.rs (extends queue from MT-184) with `FairScheduler`, `PriorityScore`, `StarvationGuard`; integration tests under src/backend/handshake_core/tests/mt_scheduler_tests.rs.
unblock_conditions: MT-184 MicroTaskJob primitive provides the rows the scheduler orders.
implementation_notes: Per spec §6.7 swarm-agent harness (lines 6793+) concurrency-invariant principle: a fair scheduler must prevent any single WP, any single escalation tier, or any single LoRA family from monopolizing executor capacity. `FairScheduler::next_for_executor(executor_kind, capabilities, now: SystemTime) -> Option<MicroTaskJob>` computes priority as: `priority = base_tier_weight(escalation_tier) + age_boost(now - created_at_utc) + fairness_penalty(per_wp_recent_claims)`. base_tier_weight: HardGate=1000 (always first), T32B=100, T13BAlt=80, T13B=60, T7BAlt=40, T7B=20. age_boost: +1 per minute waiting (caps at +200 so it can eventually outweigh tier weight, preventing indefinite starvation). fairness_penalty: -50 per recent claim by the same wp_id in the last 60s (cap at -200). Tie-break: FIFO on created_at_utc. The scheduler query uses a Postgres CTE that computes priority server-side and SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED LIMIT 1 (no in-memory re-ranking — avoids race between query and claim). `StarvationGuard` is a metrics emitter: any job whose age > `starvation_threshold_secs` (default 600s) emits FR-EVT-MT-STARVED { job_id, wp_id, age_secs } once per threshold crossing; the diagnostics panel surfaces these. Tests: (a) inject 100 fresh T7B jobs + 1 old T32B job — old job claimed within first 5 picks regardless of fresh job order; (b) inject 50 jobs from same wp_id + 5 from other wp_id — other wp_id jobs claimed proportionally; (c) starvation threshold crossing emits exactly one FR event per crossing (not per query).
risk_if_missed: Without starvation prevention, a steady stream of high-tier jobs from one WP starves low-tier jobs from another WP indefinitely — folded stub WP-1-Multi-Model-Orchestration-Lifecycle-Telemetry intent (safe parallelism) is violated.
validator_focus: Priority computed server-side in a single CTE (no out-of-band re-ranking race); FR-EVT-MT-STARVED emitted exactly once per threshold crossing per job; tie-break deterministic via FIFO.

minimum_controls: Server-side priority CTE proven by EXPLAIN ANALYZE test asserting no client-side re-rank.
Starvation metric is monotonic (no flapping) via 'last_emitted_at_utc' watermark per job.
Fairness penalty key (per_wp_recent_claims) computed from kernel_micro_task_job table rows in last 60s window, not from in-memory counter (survives process restart).
